speaker: HERMIONE
text: Take the boy to you. He so troubles me

speaker: HERMIONE
text: ’Tis past enduring.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LADY
text: Come, my gracious lord,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LADY
text: Shall I be your playfellow?

speaker: MAMILLIUS
text: No, I’ll none of you.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LADY
text: Why, my sweet lord?

speaker: MAMILLIUS
text: You’ll kiss me hard and speak to me as if

speaker: MAMILLIUS
text: I were a baby still.—I love you better.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LADY
text: And why so, my lord?

speaker: MAMILLIUS
text: Not for because

speaker: MAMILLIUS
text: Your brows are blacker—yet black brows, they say,

speaker: MAMILLIUS
text: Become some women best, so that there be not

speaker: MAMILLIUS
text: Too much hair there, but in a semicircle,

speaker: MAMILLIUS
text: Or a half-moon made with a pen.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LADY
text: Who taught this?

speaker: MAMILLIUS
text: I learned it out of women’s faces.—Pray now,

speaker: MAMILLIUS
text: What color are your eyebrows?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LADY
text: Blue, my lord.

speaker: MAMILLIUS
text: Nay, that’s a mock. I have seen a lady’s nose

speaker: MAMILLIUS
text: That has been blue, but not her eyebrows.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LADY
text: Hark ye,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LADY
text: The Queen your mother rounds apace. We shall

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LADY
text: Present our services to a fine new prince

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LADY
text: One of these days, and then you’d wanton with us

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LADY
text: If we would have you.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LADY
text: She is spread of late

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LADY
text: Into a goodly bulk. Good time encounter her!

speaker: HERMIONE
text: What wisdom stirs amongst you?—Come, sir, now

speaker: HERMIONE
text: I am for you again. Pray you sit by us,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: And tell ’s a tale.

speaker: MAMILLIUS
text: Merry or sad shall ’t be?

speaker: HERMIONE
text: As merry as you will.

speaker: MAMILLIUS
text: A sad tale’s best for winter. I have one

speaker: MAMILLIUS
text: Of sprites and goblins.

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Let’s have that, good sir.

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Come on, sit down. Come on, and do your best

speaker: HERMIONE
text: To fright me with your sprites. You’re powerful at it.

speaker: MAMILLIUS
text: There was a man—

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Nay, come sit down, then on.

speaker: MAMILLIUS
text: Dwelt by a churchyard. I will tell it softly,

speaker: MAMILLIUS
text: Yond crickets shall not hear it.

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Come on then, and give ’t me in mine ear.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Was he met there? His train? Camillo with him?

speaker: LORD
text: Behind the tuft of pines I met them. Never

speaker: LORD
text: Saw I men scour so on their way. I eyed them

speaker: LORD
text: Even to their ships.

speaker: LEONTES
text: How blest am I

speaker: LEONTES
text: In my just censure, in my true opinion!

speaker: LEONTES
text: Alack, for lesser knowledge! How accursed

speaker: LEONTES
text: In being so blest! There may be in the cup

speaker: LEONTES
text: A spider steeped, and one may drink, depart,

speaker: LEONTES
text: And yet partake no venom, for his knowledge

speaker: LEONTES
text: Is not infected; but if one present

speaker: LEONTES
text: Th’ abhorred ingredient to his eye, make known

speaker: LEONTES
text: How he hath drunk, he cracks his gorge, his sides,

speaker: LEONTES
text: With violent hefts. I have drunk, and seen the spider.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Camillo was his help in this, his pander.

speaker: LEONTES
text: There is a plot against my life, my crown.

speaker: LEONTES
text: All’s true that is mistrusted. That false villain

speaker: LEONTES
text: Whom I employed was pre-employed by him.

speaker: LEONTES
text: He has discovered my design, and I

speaker: LEONTES
text: Remain a pinched thing, yea, a very trick

speaker: LEONTES
text: For them to play at will. How came the posterns

speaker: LEONTES
text: So easily open?

speaker: LORD
text: By his great authority,

speaker: LORD
text: Which often hath no less prevailed than so

speaker: LORD
text: On your command.

speaker: LEONTES
text: I know ’t too well.

speaker: LEONTES
text:
To
                 
                Hermione
                .
                 
              Give me the boy. I am glad you did not nurse him.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Though he does bear some signs of me, yet you

speaker: LEONTES
text: Have too much blood in him.

speaker: HERMIONE
text: What is this? Sport?

speaker: LEONTES
text: Bear the boy hence. He shall not come about her.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Away with him, and let her sport herself

speaker: LEONTES
text:
With that she’s big with,
                 
                (
                to
                 
                Hermione
                )
               for ’tis Polixenes

speaker: LEONTES
text: Has made thee swell thus.

speaker: HERMIONE
text: But I’d say he had not,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: And I’ll be sworn you would believe my saying,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Howe’er you lean to th’ nayward.

speaker: LEONTES
text: You, my lords,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Look on her, mark her well. Be but about

speaker: LEONTES
text: To say “She is a goodly lady,” and

speaker: LEONTES
text: The justice of your hearts will thereto add

speaker: LEONTES
text: “’Tis pity she’s not honest, honorable.”

speaker: LEONTES
text: Praise her but for this her without-door form,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Which on my faith deserves high speech, and straight

speaker: LEONTES
text: The shrug, the “hum,” or “ha,” these petty brands

speaker: LEONTES
text: That calumny doth use—O, I am out,

speaker: LEONTES
text: That mercy does, for calumny will sear

speaker: LEONTES
text: Virtue itself—these shrugs, these “hum”s and “ha”s,

speaker: LEONTES
text: When you have said she’s goodly, come between

speaker: LEONTES
text: Ere you can say she’s honest. But be ’t known,

speaker: LEONTES
text: From him that has most cause to grieve it should be,

speaker: LEONTES
text: She’s an adult’ress.

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Should a villain say so,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: The most replenished villain in the world,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: He were as much more villain. You, my lord,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Do but mistake.

speaker: LEONTES
text: You have mistook, my lady,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Polixenes for Leontes. O thou thing,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Which I’ll not call a creature of thy place

speaker: LEONTES
text: Lest barbarism, making me the precedent,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Should a like language use to all degrees,

speaker: LEONTES
text: And mannerly distinguishment leave out

speaker: LEONTES
text: Betwixt the prince and beggar.—I have said

speaker: LEONTES
text: She’s an adult’ress; I have said with whom.

speaker: LEONTES
text: More, she’s a traitor, and Camillo is

speaker: LEONTES
text: A federary with her, and one that knows

speaker: LEONTES
text: What she should shame to know herself

speaker: LEONTES
text: But with her most vile principal: that she’s

speaker: LEONTES
text: A bed-swerver, even as bad as those

speaker: LEONTES
text: That vulgars give bold’st titles; ay, and privy

speaker: LEONTES
text: To this their late escape.

speaker: HERMIONE
text: No, by my life,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Privy to none of this. How will this grieve you,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: When you shall come to clearer knowledge, that

speaker: HERMIONE
text: You thus have published me! Gentle my lord,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: You scarce can right me throughly then to say

speaker: HERMIONE
text: You did mistake.

speaker: LEONTES
text: No. If I mistake

speaker: LEONTES
text: In those foundations which I build upon,

speaker: LEONTES
text: The center is not big enough to bear

speaker: LEONTES
text: A schoolboy’s top.—Away with her to prison.

speaker: LEONTES
text: He who shall speak for her is afar off guilty

speaker: LEONTES
text: But that he speaks.

speaker: HERMIONE
text: There’s some ill planet reigns.

speaker: HERMIONE
text: I must be patient till the heavens look

speaker: HERMIONE
text: With an aspect more favorable. Good my lords,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: I am not prone to weeping, as our sex

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Commonly are, the want of which vain dew

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Perchance shall dry your pities. But I have

speaker: HERMIONE
text: That honorable grief lodged here which burns

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Worse than tears drown. Beseech you all, my lords,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: With thoughts so qualified as your charities

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Shall best instruct you, measure me; and so

speaker: HERMIONE
text: The King’s will be performed.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Shall I be heard?

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Who is ’t that goes with me? Beseech your Highness

speaker: HERMIONE
text: My women may be with me, for you see

speaker: HERMIONE
text: My plight requires it.—Do not weep, good fools;

speaker: HERMIONE
text: There is no cause. When you shall know your mistress

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Has deserved prison, then abound in tears

speaker: HERMIONE
text: As I come out. This action I now go on

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Is for my better grace.—Adieu, my lord.

speaker: HERMIONE
text: I never wished to see you sorry; now

speaker: HERMIONE
text: I trust I shall.—My women, come; you have leave.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Go, do our bidding. Hence!

speaker: LORD
text: Beseech your Highness, call the Queen again.

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: Be certain what you do, sir, lest your justice

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: Prove violence, in the which three great ones suffer:

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: Yourself, your queen, your son.

speaker: LORD
text: For her, my lord,

speaker: LORD
text: I dare my life lay down—and will do ’t, sir,

speaker: LORD
text: Please you t’ accept it—that the Queen is spotless

speaker: LORD
text: I’ th’ eyes of heaven, and to you—I mean

speaker: LORD
text: In this which you accuse her.

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: If it prove

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: She’s otherwise, I’ll keep my stables where

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: I lodge my wife. I’ll go in couples with her;

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: Than when I feel and see her, no farther trust her.

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: For every inch of woman in the world,

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: Ay, every dram of woman’s flesh, is false,

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: If she be.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Hold your peaces.

speaker: LORD
text: Good my lord—

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: It is for you we speak, not for ourselves.

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: You are abused, and by some putter-on

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: That will be damned for ’t. Would I knew the villain!

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: I would land-damn him. Be she honor-flawed,

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: I have three daughters—the eldest is eleven;

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: The second and the third, nine and some five;

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: If this prove true, they’ll pay for ’t. By mine honor,

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: I’ll geld ’em all; fourteen they shall not see

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: To bring false generations. They are co-heirs,

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: And I had rather glib myself than they

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: Should not produce fair issue.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Cease. No more.

speaker: LEONTES
text: You smell this business with a sense as cold

speaker: LEONTES
text: As is a dead man’s nose. But I do see ’t and feel ’t,

speaker: LEONTES
text: As you feel doing thus, and see withal

speaker: LEONTES
text: The instruments that feel.

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: If it be so,

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: We need no grave to bury honesty.

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: There’s not a grain of it the face to sweeten

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: Of the whole dungy Earth.

speaker: LEONTES
text: What? Lack I credit?

speaker: LORD
text: I had rather you did lack than I, my lord,

speaker: LORD
text: Upon this ground. And more it would content me

speaker: LORD
text: To have her honor true than your suspicion,

speaker: LORD
text: Be blamed for ’t how you might.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Why, what need we

speaker: LEONTES
text: Commune with you of this, but rather follow

speaker: LEONTES
text: Our forceful instigation? Our prerogative

speaker: LEONTES
text: Calls not your counsels, but our natural goodness

speaker: LEONTES
text: Imparts this, which if you—or stupefied

speaker: LEONTES
text: Or seeming so in skill—cannot or will not

speaker: LEONTES
text: Relish a truth like us, inform yourselves

speaker: LEONTES
text: We need no more of your advice. The matter,

speaker: LEONTES
text: The loss, the gain, the ord’ring on ’t is all

speaker: LEONTES
text: Properly ours.

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: And I wish, my liege,

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: You had only in your silent judgment tried it,

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: Without more overture.

speaker: LEONTES
text: How could that be?

speaker: LEONTES
text: Either thou art most ignorant by age,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Or thou wert born a fool. Camillo’s flight,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Added to their familiarity—

speaker: LEONTES
text: Which was as gross as ever touched conjecture,

speaker: LEONTES
text: That lacked sight only, naught for approbation

speaker: LEONTES
text: But only seeing, all other circumstances

speaker: LEONTES
text: Made up to th’ deed—doth push on this proceeding.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Yet, for a greater confirmation—

speaker: LEONTES
text: For in an act of this importance ’twere

speaker: LEONTES
text: Most piteous to be wild—I have dispatched in post

speaker: LEONTES
text: To sacred Delphos, to Apollo’s temple,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Cleomenes and Dion, whom you know

speaker: LEONTES
text: Of stuffed sufficiency. Now from the oracle

speaker: LEONTES
text: They will bring all, whose spiritual counsel had

speaker: LEONTES
text: Shall stop or spur me. Have I done well?

speaker: LORD
text: Well done, my lord.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Though I am satisfied and need no more

speaker: LEONTES
text: Than what I know, yet shall the oracle

speaker: LEONTES
text: Give rest to th’ minds of others, such as he

speaker: LEONTES
text: Whose ignorant credulity will not

speaker: LEONTES
text: Come up to th’ truth. So have we thought it good

speaker: LEONTES
text: From our free person she should be confined,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Lest that the treachery of the two fled hence

speaker: LEONTES
text: Be left her to perform. Come, follow us.

speaker: LEONTES
text: We are to speak in public, for this business

speaker: LEONTES
text: Will raise us all.

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: To laughter, as I take it,

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: If the good truth were known.
